LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LORI REESE-BROWN'S MOTION TO REINSTATE CASE ON CALENDAR DUE TO DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO PROVIDE COURTESY FOPIES UNDER LOCAL RULE 2.7(B). PLAINTIFF LORI REESE-BROWN'S MOTION TO REINSTATE THIS CASE ON CALENDAR IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS UNNECESSARY AND MOOT. THIS CASE, AS DISTINCT FROM A MOTION OR DEMURRER FILED IN THIS CASE, HAS NEVER BEEN TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. THE CASE REMAINS ON THE CIVIL ACTIVE LIST. MOREOVER, THE DEMURRER OF MR. COWAN WHICH WAS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR WHICH PRESUMABLY PRECIPITATED THIS MOTION, HAS BEEN RE-NOTICED FOR AND WILL BE HEARD ON MARCH 24, 2025. VISITING JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Disclosure Of Insurance Information
Off Calendar Jun-30-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Lori Reese-Brown, in propria persona, Tel: (650) 269-2328, Plaintiff.
Timothy S. Lam, Esq., of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney, Tel:(415) 788-1900, for Defendant John E. Cowan.

Hearing is held. Plaintiff contests the tentative ruling. The matter argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

2 - Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Disclosure of Insurance Information is DENIED.

On April 24, 2025, the court granted Plaintiff's motion pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.4 to stay the proceedings pending completion of the arbitration. Once such a stay is issued, discovery disputes are heard not by the court, but the arbitrator. (See Cinel v. Christopher (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 759, 769.) Thus, this motion, which seeks adjudication of a discovery dispute, is denied.

Defendant's motion for sanctions is denied.

Order is Signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT JOHN COWAN'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Lori Reese-Brown, in propria persona, (650) 269-2328, Plaintiff.
Timothy S. Lam, Esq., of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney, (415) 788-1900, for Defendant John E. Cowan.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. Off calendar in light of the ruling on plaintiff Lori Reese-Brown's motion to stay proceedings, compel mediation/arbitration, and strike defendant's demurrer as an abuse of process. The parties are required to mediate their disputes and, if unsuccessful in reaching a settlement through mediation, are required to arbitrate their disputes. This case is stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration.

At Plaintiff's request, a schedule regarding the deadlines to mediate and/or arbitrate are discussed and confirmed. Plaintiff is to submit a proposed order to the court via email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org which conforms to the dates discussed and confirmed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/HK)

***After hearing (4/22/2025): the deadlines for mediation and aribtration are confirmed as follows: Mediation should occur within thirty (30) days of the date of the order. If mediation is unsuccessful, the parties should arbitrate within thirty (30) days afterwards.

MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar Mar-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF LORI REESE-BROWN'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Lori Reese-Brown, in propria persona, (650) 269-2328, Plaintiff.
Timothy S. Lam, Esq., of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney, (415) 788-1900, for Defendant John E. Cowan.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Lori Reese-Brown's motion to stay proceedings, compel mediation/arbitration, and strike defendant's demurrer as an abuse of process is granted as to the portions of the motion seeking a stay of this case and compelling the parties to mediate and, if necessary, arbitrate their disputes and is off calendar as to the portion of the motion seeking to strike the demurrer. Ms. Reese-Brown has shown that this motion was timely filed per the notice requirements of CCP 1005. Ms. Reese-Brown has shown that her agreement with Mr. Brown provides for non-binding mediation and, if no settlement is achieved through mediation, binding arbitration. The failure of Ms. Reese-Brown to put her initials in the last paragraph of section 10 of the agreement does not invalidate the mediation and arbitration provisions of the agreement. Nothing in the agreement states that the failure to put initials in that paragraph invalidates the mediation and arbitration language of the agreement. To the extent that the agreement is ambiguous on this point, the parties do not provide any extrinsic evidence and thus the California policy of resolving doubts in favor of arbitration and the contract interpretation doctrine of construing an agreement against the drafter both strongly favor an interpretation of the agreement that the mediation and arbitration language apply notwithstanding the failure of Ms. Reese-Brown to put her initials in the last paragraph of section 10 of the agreement. Ms. Reese-Brown has not waived her right to non-binding mediation and arbitration. Roughly concurrent with her initiating this action Ms. Reese-Brown sent a letter to Mr. Cowan requesting mediation per the parties' agreement, which shows her intent to retain her contractual rights to mediation and arbitration. Mr. Wowan has not submitted any evidence that Ms. Reese-Brown has, by words or conduct, intentionally relinquished or abandoned her rights to mediate and arbitrate her disputes with Mr. Cowan and, as a result, Mr. Cowan has not satisfied his burden of showing waiver by any standard, much less the required high standard of clear and convincing evidence. (Quach v. California Commerce Club, Inc. (2024) 16 Cal. 5th 562, 584-587).

Mr. Cowan's request for this court to decide his demurrer to the first amended complaint is denied. Doing so is inconsistent with the parties' agreement for non-binding mediation and arbitration.

At Plaintiff's request, a schedule regarding the deadlines to mediate and/or arbitrate are discussed and confirmed. Plaintiff is to submit a proposed order to the court via email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org which conforms to the dates discussed and confirmed in open court and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/HK)

***After hearing (4/22/2025): the deadlines for mediation and aribtration are confirmed as follows: Mediation should occur within thirty (30) days of the date of the order. If mediation is unsuccessful, the parties should arbitrate within thirty (30) days afterwards.
